repo_notes.content: Developer documentation only. Mirror the repo structure and module boundaries (packages/, apps/, meeseeks_ha_conversation/) with minimal redundancy. Every page must include a meaningful Mermaid diagram that matches the page purpose. Name exact classes, functions, and module paths. Clarify that the API exposes polling; the CLI runs the runtime in-process (no polling); the web console connects via the REST API. Focus on system behavior, abstractions, and integration contracts rather than usage tips. Avoid anthropomorphic language when describing LLM behavior (use 'the model generates' not 'the AI decides').

pages.title: Overview & Repo Map
pages.purpose: Single entry point: repo goals, module boundaries (packages/, apps/, meeseeks_ha_conversation/), and top-level folder ownership. Highlights the shared-host model. New in v0.0.10: plugin system (plugins.py, agent_registry.py in meeseeks_core), Web IDE integration (ide.py/ide_routes.py in meeseeks_api), LSP code intelligence (meeseeks_tools/integration/lsp/), chat platform channel adapters (Nextcloud Talk + Email), and conversation fork/edit/regenerate.

pages.title: Core Runtime & Orchestration (meeseeks_core)
pages.purpose: How the shared core runtime and orchestration engine work: SessionRuntime as the facade, Orchestrator as the session driver, ToolUseLoop as the execution engine. Covers the single-async-loop architecture where the LLM drives all tool calls via native bind_tools.

pages.title: Session Runtime, Store & Event Log
pages.purpose: SessionRuntime facade, RunRegistry (thread tracking), SessionStore (JSONL transcripts, tags, archives, attachments). Fork-from-message: resolve_session(fork_from=id_or_tag, fork_at_ts=iso_ts) creates a new session with only events up to the given timestamp — enables edit-and-regenerate from any point in history. Per-session temporary directory as default CWD (assigned at session creation). Polling is API-only; CLI runs in-process.

pages.title: Tool-Use Loop Execution Engine
pages.purpose: ToolUseLoop.run() as the single async execution engine: message construction (_build_messages with system prompt, project instructions, context, attachments, tool guidance, skill catalog, depth guidance), LLM invocation via bind_tools, tool dispatch with concurrency-safe partitioning (_partition_tool_calls batches concurrent-safe tools vs exclusive tools), per-tool timeout (asyncio.wait_for with configurable ToolSpec.timeout), non-blocking spawn for root agent (returns immediately with agent_id), check_agents/steer_agent management tools (root-only), user steering (message_queue drain and interrupt_step check), step budget tracking with graduated enforcement (NL warnings, not kill), forced synthesis with pending children at step limit, and cancellation.

pages.title: Agent Hypervisor & Sub-Agent Lifecycle
pages.purpose: AgentHypervisor as a dual-layer autonomous system: reflexes (code-level step tracking, stall detection via background _watchdog, budget enforcement via NL warning injection) and brain (root LLM sees global agent tree via render_agent_tree(), uses check_agents and steer_agent tools). 6-state lifecycle (submitted → running → completed/failed/cancelled/rejected per A2A v1.0). AgentResult as structured Communication Units (content, status, steps_used, summary, warnings, artifacts). Non-blocking spawn for root (epoll model — react to notifications, don't poll) vs blocking spawn for sub-agents. Bidirectional messaging (parent→child via send_message/steer_agent, child→parent via _run_child_lifecycle on completion). AgentHandle (per-agent runtime state with progress_note, result, message_queue). 3-phase graceful cleanup (cancel → wait → force-mark). SpawnAgentTool with filter_specs() for tool scoping.

pages.title: LLM Abstraction & Model Routing
pages.purpose: ChatModel protocol, build_chat_model(), LiteLLM multi-provider gateway, provider-qualified model names, per-role model selection (plan/tool/default), reasoning-effort configuration (reasoning_effort field with model allowlist), proxy_model_prefix (default 'openai' — prepended to bare model names when llm.api_base is set for proxy routing, configurable to match proxy's expected prefix), and model_prefers_structured_patch() (returns bool for edit tool auto-selection). specs_to_langchain_tools() for converting ToolSpecs to LLM-bindable schemas.

pages.title: Context Builder, Summaries & Compaction
pages.purpose: ContextBuilder.build() pipeline: transcript loading, event filtering, recent event selection, attachment reading, token budget tracking, auto-compaction triggers. Token budget now accounts for tool schema + system prompt overhead (~20-25k tokens). context_compacted events emitted at both proactive (pre-limit) and reactive (post-ContextWindowExceededError) compaction — visible in Console and CLI. Mid-loop reactive compaction prevents crashes. Two-mode compaction: FULL (summarizes everything) and PARTIAL (keeps recent events verbatim). Post-compact file restoration re-injects recently referenced files within token budgets. Micro-compaction (ANSI stripping + truncation) via pre_compact hook. AgentHandle.compaction_count tracked for hypervisor tree visibility.

pages.title: Permissions, Hooks & Tool Execution Gates
pages.purpose: PermissionPolicy (allow/deny/ask rules, fnmatch on tool_id and operation), approval callbacks (auto_approve, config-driven, interactive). HookManager with error isolation (all hooks wrapped in try/except — failing hook logs warning but doesn't block execution). Lifecycle hooks: on_session_start, on_session_end, on_compact. External command hooks via HooksConfig with fnmatch-based tool matcher filtering. Pre/post tool use hooks. Agent lifecycle hooks (on_agent_start, on_agent_stop).

pages.title: Data Models, Event Types & Payload Schemas
pages.purpose: ActionStep (tool_id/operation/tool_input), PlanStep, TaskQueue, OrchestrationState, EventRecord, ToolResult (content/success/error/truncated), and typed payload schemas (UserPayload, AssistantPayload, ToolResultPayload with kind discriminator, ActionPlanPayload, CompletionPayload, PermissionPayload, SubAgentPayload with lifecycle state, AgentMessagePayload). Event type discrimination and JSONL serialization. Structured tool results with kind field ('shell', 'diff', 'file', 'dir') for frontend routing.

pages.title: Skills System
pages.purpose: SkillRegistry discovers SKILL.md files from ~/.claude/skills/ and .claude/skills/. Plugin skill loading via load_extra_dir(path) (adds skills from plugin directories) and load_command_file(path) (loads skills from plugin commands.json). No-override semantics: installed plugins cannot shadow user-defined skills. Skill catalog injected into system prompt for LLM auto-invocation via activate_skill. User /skill-name invocations detected in Orchestrator. Skills scope tools via allowed-tools (filter_specs()). merge_plugin_hooks() translates Claude Code plugin hooks.json format into HookManager callbacks with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} substitution.

pages.title: Tool Layer (meeseeks_tools)
pages.purpose: Tool layer boundaries: local tools (edit, shell, file read/list), MCP tools, LSP code intelligence. AbstractTool base class with get_state/set_state/run dispatch. MockSpeaker result container. Tool results use kind-discriminated dicts (kind: 'shell'|'diff'|'file'|'dir'|'lsp') for structured frontend rendering. LSP tool (lsp_tool in meeseeks_tools/integration/lsp/): graceful absence when pygls/lsprotocol not installed.

pages.title: Tool Registry & Spec System
pages.purpose: ToolRegistry, ToolSpec (tool_id, kind, metadata with schema/capabilities/reflect, factory for lazy instantiation), filter_specs() for allowlist/denylist filtering (shared by Orchestrator, SpawnAgentTool, and skills), specs_to_langchain_tools() conversion, and tool disabling with reasons. Conditional tool registration pattern: _edit_tool_spec_and_manifest() reads AgentConfig.edit_tool and returns matching ToolSpec + manifest entry for both _default_registry() and _built_in_manifest_entries() — single conditional, two consumers. Capabilities metadata (e.g. 'file_write') enables intent-based tool filtering in planning.

pages.title: Configurable Edit Tool Mechanism
pages.purpose: Two edit tool mechanisms selectable via AgentConfig.edit_tool: 'search_replace_block' (Aider-style) and 'structured_patch' (per-file exact). When edit_tool is empty (default), ToolUseLoop._configured_edit_tool_id() auto-selects via llm.model_prefers_structured_patch(model_name) — checking the model name string against a known list of structured-patch-preferring models. Explicit config always overrides auto-detection. Shared utilities in edit_common.py. Both tools output {'kind': 'diff'} for DiffCard compatibility. End-to-end consistency: ToolSpec bundles schema + prompt_path + factory.

pages.title: Local Tools (File Edit, Shell, Read)
pages.purpose: AiderEditBlockTool (SEARCH/REPLACE format with aider_bridge parsing, fuzzy matching, diff generation), FileEditTool (per-file exact string replacement, strict matching, ambiguity rejection), shared edit_common.py utilities (path resolution, before-state reading, unified diff computation via difflib, canonical result formatting). AiderShellTool (command execution with structured {kind: 'shell'} results including exit_code, stdout, stderr, duration_ms). AiderReadFileTool and AiderListDirTool (read-only tools with {kind: 'file'}/{kind: 'dir'} results). All tools use resolve_safe_path() to prevent directory traversal. Approval gating for set operations.

pages.title: MCP Tool Discovery & Schema Validation
pages.purpose: MCP config (configs/mcp.json global, .mcp.json project-scoped), server discovery with project CWD propagation, schema normalization, per-server failure isolation, _coerce_mcp_tool_input validation, and manifest caching. MCPConnectionPool (persistent MCP connections with memoization, automatic reconnection after 3 consecutive errors, per-request 60s timeout, config change detection). MCPToolRunner uses pool as primary path with legacy one-shot client as fallback.

pages.title: REST API (meeseeks_api)
pages.purpose: Flask-restx API: route structure, authentication (X-API-Key from docker.env MASTER_API_TOKEN), CORS, how handlers bind to SessionRuntime, NotificationStore, ShareStore, and the tool registry. Config API endpoints for reading/patching app.json at runtime.

pages.title: API Endpoints, Polling & Steering
pages.purpose: Session CRUD, async query execution (start_async + 202), event polling (/events?after=), SSE streaming (/stream), user steering (/message, /interrupt), agent tree (/agents), attachments, share tokens, export, notifications, config API (GET/PATCH /config), legacy sync /query. Plugin management: GET /api/plugins, GET/POST /api/plugins/marketplace, DELETE /api/plugins/<name>. Web IDE lifecycle: POST/DELETE /api/sessions/{id}/ide, POST /api/sessions/{id}/ide/extend. Git diff: GET /api/sessions/{id}/git-diff?scope=uncommitted|branch. Session fork: POST /api/sessions/{id}/fork with from_ts, model, compact, tag params. Includes allowed_tools passthrough from context.mcp_tools through SessionRuntime → Orchestrator → ToolUseLoop.

pages.title: Web Console (meeseeks_console)
pages.purpose: React 18 + Vite + Tailwind CSS frontend. Session management with polling (1s interval). Conversation timeline with turn grouping. ReviewPane accordion: scope selector (last-turn / uncommitted / branch) backed by GET /api/sessions/{id}/git-diff, replaces old single-file DiffView. ForkFromHereButton and inline edit on user messages for edit-and-regenerate with model override. PluginsView: marketplace browser + install/uninstall. IdeLoader: per-session code-server container launch with floral background animation. <Button> primitive (components/ui/Button.tsx): all buttons use this with variants (primary/secondary/ghost/danger) and sizes. Session header redesigned to one-row layout with IDE capsule and overflow menu. useWebIdeEnabled(), useIdeStatus() hooks. MCP tool/skill/project pickers with TTL cache (60s). No state management or UI component libraries.

pages.title: Console UI Components
pages.purpose: Tool card hierarchy: LogEventCard (base — expand/collapse, accent border), TerminalCard (dark shell card), DiffCard (always-dark, diff2html, file icons, collapsed preview). Shared primitives: CopyButton (visible at rest at 50% opacity), Badge, StatusBadge, Popover, MarkdownContent. <Button> primitive in components/ui/Button.tsx (variants, sizes, iconOnly). ReviewPane (ReviewPane.tsx): accordion file list with 3-scope selector (last-turn/uncommitted/branch), useGitDiff hook, extracts non-component DiffCard utils to utils/diffCard.ts. PluginsView (PluginsView.tsx): installed plugin table + marketplace browser with search/category filter. Edit-mode MessageBubble: textarea fills max-w-[85%], dashed accent border, info note below. Failed file edits render as DiffCard with error state (synthesized from tool_input). buildLogs() and parseStructuredResult() for kind-discriminated routing.

pages.title: Terminal CLI (meeseeks_cli)
pages.purpose: CLI flow: run_cli entry point, Rich-based rendering (plan checklist, tool result cards, response panel), AgentDisplayManager + Rich Live for agent tree display during execution (collapsible tree, spinner, elapsed time, keybindings), DialogFactory (Textual-based interactive prompts), in-process SessionRuntime usage (sync, no polling), slash commands, and session management. Approval styles: inline (plain prompt), textual (TUI dialog), aider (diff-style).

pages.title: Home Assistant Integration (meeseeks_ha_conversation)
pages.purpose: HA Conversation integration: async HTTP client calling POST /api/query on the Meeseeks API, MeeseeksApiClient, response parsing, and config flow.

pages.title: Configuration & Runtime Settings
pages.purpose: AppConfig hierarchy: RuntimeConfig, LLMConfig (reasoning_effort, reasoning_effort_models, proxy_model_prefix), ContextConfig, TokenBudgetConfig, ReflectionConfig, LangfuseConfig, HomeAssistantConfig, PermissionsConfig, CLIConfig, ChatConfig, APIConfig, AgentConfig (edit_tool, max_depth, max_concurrent, max_iters, sub_agent_max_steps, default_denied_tools, allowed_models, web_ide: WebIdeConfig), HooksConfig, ProjectConfig, PluginsConfig (registry_paths, marketplaces, install_path with resolver methods). Configs/app.json and configs/mcp.json resolution with MEESEEKS_HOME discovery chain. WebIdeConfig (enabled, image, port, ttl_hours, data_dir). PluginsConfig.resolve_registry_paths() / resolve_install_dir() / resolve_marketplace_dirs(). Conditional component enabling via evaluate() + ComponentStatus. Field validators with normalization. ConfigDict(extra='forbid').

pages.title: CI/CD, Docker & Docs Pipeline
pages.purpose: GitHub Actions workflows: console.yml (lint + test + build on console changes), docker-buildx.yml (multi-arch builds for base/api/console to ghcr.io on release/* branches), lint.yml (Python ruff + mypy), coverage.yml (pytest + codecov), docs.yml (mkdocs), uv-lock-refresh.yml (weekly dependency updates). Dockerfile structure: base (Python 3.11 + system packages + Node.js + uv), api (multi-stage on base with Gunicorn), console (multi-stage node:20-slim → nginx:alpine). Docker Compose with host networking, env_file, post-init script mechanism (docker/entrypoint-api.sh sources init.d scripts), volume mounts for configs and project dirs. pull_policy: always on images — use --pull never for local builds.

pages.title: Testing Patterns & Quality Gates
pages.purpose: Integration-first test patterns: fake tools, AsyncMock for model.ainvoke, full-turn harness tests, event stream assertions. 600+ tests across core, tools, API, and integration. New test files: test_plugins.py (discovery/manifest/marketplace/install/uninstall), test_plugin_hooks.py (hook format translation), test_plugin_integration.py (end-to-end plugin session init), test_agent_registry.py (frontmatter parsing, CC tool name mapping, agent_type lookup), test_session_runtime.py (fork_at_ts), test_ide_manager.py + test_ide_routes.py (Web IDE lifecycle, chown/exited/proxy). Pre-commit hooks (ruff, mypy, console-lint). asyncio.run() in sync tests (not pytest-asyncio). Git tests in temp dirs must set user.name/email for CI.

pages.title: Tracing, Logging & Diagnostics
pages.purpose: Loguru-based logging with get_logger(), session_log_context for per-session log capture. Langfuse tracing (session-scoped traces, trace names: meeseeks-tool-use, meeseeks-task-master, meeseeks-context). Session investigation methodology: MongoDB transcript (authoritative event log) → Langfuse traces (LLM conversation chain with full system prompts and tool bindings) → API config (app.json, docker.env) → Docker environment. Error propagation through ToolUseLoop._safe_execute with per-tool timeout and structured AgentError.

pages.title: Building a New Client
pages.purpose: Step-by-step walkthrough for extending Meeseeks with a new interface: initialize core services (load_registry with conditional edit tool, permissions, SessionRuntime), resolve sessions, handle slash commands and skill invocations, choose sync vs async execution, consume session events (including kind-discriminated tool results for frontend rendering), project instructions loading mechanism (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md with hierarchical discovery from user/project/rules/local levels, meeseeks:noload marker, subtree discovery for nested instructions and skills).

pages.title: Plugin System & Marketplace
pages.purpose: PluginsConfig (registry_paths, marketplaces, install_path). plugins.py: PluginManifest dataclass (name, version, description, source, skills, commands, agents, hooks, mcp_servers), PluginComponents, substitute_plugin_vars() with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} substitution, _deep_substitute(), parse_plugin_manifest(), discover_plugin_components(), discover_installed_plugins(), discover_marketplace_plugins() (clones git repos for standalone/Docker), install_plugin() (git clone or URL download), uninstall_plugin(). Security: path traversal guard on all install paths, KeyError safety on missing manifest fields, MCP format unwrap (mcpServers key normalization). load_all_plugin_components() is called during Orchestrator session init — fans out skills (SkillRegistry.load_extra_dir), hooks (merge_plugin_hooks), agent definitions (AgentRegistry.register), MCP servers (extra_servers param to get_merged_mcp_config). CLI: /plugins, /plugins marketplace, /plugins install <name>, /plugins uninstall <name>. Auto-reconcile: missing enabled plugins are re-enabled on container startup.

pages.title: Agent Definition Registry
pages.purpose: AgentDef frozen dataclass (name, description, body, tools, model, disallowed_tools). AgentRegistry with no-override semantics (installed plugins cannot shadow user-defined agents). parse_agent_file() reads YAML frontmatter from markdown files: name, description, tools (CC tool name mapping via CC_TOOL_MAP), model, disallowedTools. CC_TOOL_MAP translates Claude Code tool names to Meeseeks tool_ids (e.g., 'Bash' -> 'shell_tool', 'Edit' -> edit tool). map_cc_tool_names() applies the mapping. agent_type field in SPAWN_AGENT_SCHEMA: when provided, SpawnAgentTool looks up the AgentDef in AgentRegistry and applies body/tools/model overrides. Agent catalog injected into system prompt alongside skill catalog.

pages.title: Chat Platform Adapters
pages.purpose: ChannelAdapter Protocol (channels/base.py): verify_request(request) -> bool, parse_inbound(request) -> InboundMessage | None, send_response(session_id, message) -> None, system_context property -> str. ChannelRegistry for adapter lookup. DeduplicationGuard (TTL-based replay protection). Shared _process_inbound() pipeline in routes.py: dedup -> mention gate (requires_mention()) -> session resolve (tag_session/resolve_tag) -> slash commands (@command registry: /help, /usage, /new, /switch-project) -> LLM invocation. Webhook endpoint: POST /api/webhooks/<platform> (HMAC auth). Nextcloud Talk (nextcloud_talk.py): HMAC-SHA256 verification, ActivityStreams 2.0 parsing, OCS Bot API response with replyTo. Session tags: nextcloud-talk:room:<token> and nextcloud-talk:thread:<room>:<thread_id>. Email (email_adapter.py): EmailAdapter (IMAP parse, SMTP reply, markdown->HTML via mistune), EmailPoller (daemon thread, configurable poll_interval_seconds). Session tags: email:thread:<sender>:<root-msg-id>. Email access control: allowed_senders allowlist + @Meeseeks mention in multi-party threads. Channel sessions are standard API sessions (MongoDB/JSON, visible in console, Langfuse-traced).

pages.title: Web IDE Integration
pages.purpose: Opt-in per-session code-server containers gated by agent.web_ide.enabled. IdeInstance frozen dataclass (session_id, container_name, port, expires_at, password, status). IdeStore (MongoDB wrapper with unique index on session_id). IdeManager (ide.py): spawn() via Docker SDK (code-server image, bridge network meeseeks-ide, watchdog sh inline that polls a deadline file and kills itself when expired), reconnect() checks _container_running() (status == 'running' — not just NotFound), extend() rewrites deadline file, stop() removes container. 2s TTL-cached health probe through ide-proxy. ide-proxy (docker/nginx-ide-proxy.conf): nginx sidecar on meeseeks-ide bridge, routes /ide/<session_id>/ via Docker embedded DNS (127.0.0.11), strips URI prefix via rewrite before proxy_pass. Three-way bugfix: (1) chown in entrypoint-api.sh for /tmp/meeseeks-ide bind mount, (2) _container_running() catches exited containers and safe_remove before respawn, (3) nginx rewrite strips /ide/<sid>/ prefix before forwarding. Password returned only on POST. ide_routes.py: POST/DELETE /api/sessions/{id}/ide, POST /api/sessions/{id}/ide/extend (hours XOR expires_at). Console: IdeLoader page (POST -> poll -> hidden form submit to /login), useIdeStatus (visibility-aware polling), useWebIdeEnabled (reads getConfig() promise).

pages.title: LSP & Code Intelligence
pages.purpose: Native LSP integration backed by pygls and lsprotocol (gracefully absent when not installed — LSP_AVAILABLE flag). LSPTool (tool_id: lsp_tool): operations: diagnostics (passive — injected after every file edit via _append_lsp_feedback hook in ToolUseLoop), definition (go-to-definition), references (find all references), hover (type/doc info). LSPManager (manager.py): one manager per session, lazy server spawning via shutil.which discovery, per-server subprocess lifecycle. ServerDef dataclass: command, args, language_ids, root_uri. BUILTIN_SERVERS (servers.py): pyright (Python), typescript-language-server (TS/JS), gopls (Go), rust-analyzer (Rust). Config: agent.lsp.enabled (default true) + agent.lsp.servers (per-server overrides or custom server definitions). Passive diagnostics: after every successful file edit, ToolUseLoop calls _append_lsp_feedback to inject diagnostics as a follow-up ToolMessage — zero extra LLM turns, signals errors immediately. Per-session shutdown via shutdown_lsp_managers().

pages.title: Conversation Fork, Edit & Regenerate
pages.purpose: fork_session_at() in session_runtime.py composes resolve_session(fork_from, fork_at_ts) + truncate for point-in-time branching. resolve_recovery_query() accepts replacement_text for edit-and-regenerate (replaces the user message at fork point with new text). POST /api/sessions/{id}/fork: body params from_ts (ISO timestamp), model (optional override), compact (bool), tag (string). /recover endpoint extended with edited_text and model override params. Console: ForkFromHereButton appears on hover over any turn, inline edit on user messages (textarea fills max-w-[85%], dashed accent border, info note that prior agent actions are not reverted), model override passed through fork request. CLI: /fork --at TIMESTAMP --compact and /edit command. Session created by fork is a standard session — fully visible in console, event-streamable, archivable.

page_notes.title: Overview & Repo Map
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: module map with subgraphs by top-level folder. Show Console -> API -> Runtime -> Core flow. Add: Plugin system path (plugins.py -> agent_registry.py -> session init), Web IDE path (ide.py/ide_routes.py -> Docker SDK -> code-server container), LSP path (lsp/ -> language server subprocess), Channel adapters path (channels/ -> Nextcloud Talk / Email). Include fork-from-message as a SessionRuntime capability.

page_notes.title: Core Runtime & Orchestration (meeseeks_core)
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: runtime facade flow (SessionRuntime -> Orchestrator -> ToolUseLoop) showing how CLI (sync), API (async), and Console (via API) reach the same engine. Include skills loading path.

page_notes.title: Session Runtime, Store & Event Log
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: two flows. (1) Async run: RunRegistry.start() -> thread + queue/event -> JSONL writes -> API polling. (2) Fork-from-message: resolve_session(fork_from, fork_at_ts) -> copy events up to ts -> new session_id -> start fresh. Include RunHandle fields and per-session temp dir assignment.

page_notes.title: Tool-Use Loop Execution Engine
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: ToolUseLoop step cycle (_build_messages with all 11 sections -> LLM invoke -> _partition_tool_calls -> concurrent batch execution -> collect results -> step budget check -> check steering queue/interrupt -> repeat or done). Show non-blocking spawn returning immediately with agent_id, and check_agents/steer_agent as root-only management tools.

page_notes.title: Agent Hypervisor & Sub-Agent Lifecycle
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: dual-layer diagram. Top: root LLM with check_agents/steer_agent tools seeing render_agent_tree(). Bottom: code-level reflexes (update_step on every tool call, _watchdog background task). Show 6-state lifecycle (submitted → running → terminal states). Show _run_child_lifecycle manager for non-blocking spawn with send_to_parent notification.

page_notes.title: LLM Abstraction & Model Routing
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: class diagram for ChatModel protocol and build_chat_model, showing LiteLLM provider routing with proxy_model_prefix path (empty model name -> prepend proxy_model_prefix). Include reasoning_effort configuration path. Show model_prefers_structured_patch() as a utility function queried by ToolUseLoop._configured_edit_tool_id().

page_notes.title: Context Builder, Summaries & Compaction
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: data flow from transcript -> event filtering -> recent_events + selected_events + attachment_texts -> ContextSnapshot. Token budget accounting (tool schema + system prompt overhead ~20-25k). Two compaction triggers: proactive (near limit, PARTIAL mode) and reactive (post-ContextWindowExceededError). context_compacted event emitted both paths. Show post-compact file restoration step and micro-compaction pre_compact hook.

page_notes.title: Permissions, Hooks & Tool Execution Gates
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: sequence of tool call -> HookManager.run_pre_tool_use -> PermissionPolicy.decide -> approval_callback (if ASK) -> execute -> HookManager.run_post_tool_use. Show error isolation wrapper. Side track for lifecycle hooks (on_session_start, on_session_end, on_compact) and external command hooks.

page_notes.title: Data Models, Event Types & Payload Schemas
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: class diagram for ActionStep, TaskQueue, OrchestrationState, EventRecord with discriminated union of payload types. Include the kind field on tool results (shell/diff/file/dir) and how it drives frontend component routing.

page_notes.title: Skills System
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: three skill loading paths: (1) user ~/.claude/skills/ discovery; (2) plugin load_extra_dir(path); (3) plugin load_command_file(path). All feed into SkillRegistry with no-override semantics. Show merge_plugin_hooks() translating plugin hooks.json to HookManager callbacks. Show skill catalog injection into system prompt and activate_skill invocation path.

page_notes.title: Tool Layer (meeseeks_tools)
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: ToolRegistry -> ToolSpec -> AbstractTool hierarchy. Branch to: local tools (edit tools, shell, file read/list), MCP adapters (MCPToolRunner + MCPConnectionPool), LSP tool (LSPTool -> LSPManager -> per-language server subprocess). Show lsp_tool result type 'lsp' and passive diagnostic injection path from ToolUseLoop._append_lsp_feedback hook.

page_notes.title: Tool Registry & Spec System
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: class diagram with ToolRegistry (register, list_specs, list_specs_for_mode), ToolSpec (with metadata.schema, metadata.capabilities, factory), _edit_tool_spec_and_manifest() conditional helper, filter_specs(allowed, denied), and specs_to_langchain_tools conversion.

page_notes.title: Configurable Edit Tool Mechanism
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: config-driven + auto-detect tool selection flow. Two branches: (1) explicit edit_tool config -> ToolSpec{schema, prompt_path, factory}; (2) empty edit_tool -> _configured_edit_tool_id() -> model_prefers_structured_patch(model_name) -> bool -> ToolSpec. Three parallel outputs from selected ToolSpec: bind_tools(schema) to LLM, _render_tool_guidance(prompt_path) to system message, factory() to tool instance. Show edit_common.py as shared dependency of both tools. Show format_diff_result() producing {kind: 'diff'} for DiffCard.

page_notes.title: Local Tools (File Edit, Shell, Read)
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: sequence for both edit tools: input parsing -> resolve_safe_path -> read_file_contents (before state) -> apply edit -> write file -> build_unified_diff -> format_diff_result. Show the branching: AiderEditBlockTool goes through aider_bridge for SEARCH/REPLACE parsing, FileEditTool does direct str.count/str.replace. Highlight shared edit_common.py functions.

page_notes.title: MCP Tool Discovery & Schema Validation
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: flow from configs/mcp.json + .mcp.json (project-scoped) -> MCPConnectionPool (memoized connections, 3-error reconnection) -> server discovery -> schema normalization -> _coerce_mcp_tool_input validation at execution time. Show fallback to legacy one-shot client.

page_notes.title: REST API (meeseeks_api)
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: request flow from Flask route handlers -> _require_api_key -> SessionRuntime/NotificationStore/ShareStore -> response. Show CORS after_request hook. Include config API path.

page_notes.title: API Endpoints, Polling & Steering
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: grouped endpoint map. Core session flow (POST /sessions -> start_async -> poll /events). Steering (POST /message, POST /interrupt). Plugin management (GET/POST/DELETE /api/plugins, /marketplace). Web IDE (POST/DELETE/POST /sessions/{id}/ide, /extend). Fork (POST /sessions/{id}/fork with from_ts). Git diff (GET /sessions/{id}/git-diff?scope=). Include allowed_tools passthrough path.

page_notes.title: Web Console (meeseeks_console)
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: component hierarchy (App -> HomeView/SessionDetailView). SessionDetailView: ConversationTimeline (with ForkFromHereButton on turns, inline edit on user messages) + WorkspacePanel (ReviewPane with 3-scope selector backed by useGitDiff) + InputBar (Button primitive, searchable ConfigMenu with focus glow). New routes: /plugins (PluginsView), /ide-loader/:sessionId (IdeLoader with floral animation). Data hooks: useSessions, useSessionEvents, useMcpTools, useSkills, useProjects, useConfig, useWebIdeEnabled, useIdeStatus.

page_notes.title: Console UI Components
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: component inheritance. LogEventCard as base. TerminalCard and DiffCard as specialized siblings. ReviewPane as standalone accordion (FileEntry items, 3-scope selector, useGitDiff hook). PluginsView (installed table + marketplace browser). <Button> primitive (variants: primary/secondary/ghost/danger, sizes: sm/md/lg, iconOnly mode). IdeLoader (floral SVG background + radial spotlight + backdrop-blur card). Edit-mode MessageBubble (textarea fills max-w-[85%], dashed accent border). Show data flow: EventRecord -> buildLogs(parseStructuredResult) -> kind routing -> component.

page_notes.title: Terminal CLI (meeseeks_cli)
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: sync flow CLI -> SessionRuntime.run_sync -> Orchestrator -> ToolUseLoop -> response rendering. Include AgentDisplayManager lifecycle hook bridge to Rich Live panel. Show approval_style switching (inline/textual/aider).

page_notes.title: Home Assistant Integration (meeseeks_ha_conversation)
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: HA Assist voice -> MeeseeksApiClient -> POST /api/query -> response parsing -> HA conversation response.

page_notes.title: Configuration & Runtime Settings
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: AppConfig tree showing all sub-configs. Highlight new sections: PluginsConfig (registry_paths, marketplaces, install_path, resolve_* methods), WebIdeConfig (enabled, image, ttl_hours), LLMConfig.proxy_model_prefix. Show evaluate() pattern for conditional components (HomeAssistant, Langfuse, web_ide).

page_notes.title: CI/CD, Docker & Docs Pipeline
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: pipeline flow (push/PR -> lint + mypy + pytest -> coverage -> Docker buildx for base/api/console images -> GHCR push). Separate track for mkdocs build + deploy. Include docker-compose with post-init scripts and pull_policy note.

page_notes.title: Testing Patterns & Quality Gates
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: test harness structure with fake tools + AsyncMock LLM boundary. Note new test files and coverage areas: test_plugins (20 TDD tests for manifest/install/marketplace), test_agent_registry (20 tests for frontmatter/CC mapping), test_plugin_integration (session init wiring), test_session_runtime (fork_at_ts), test_ide_manager + test_ide_routes (Web IDE, 44 new tests total). 600+ test count. Pre-commit hooks. asyncio.run() pattern.

page_notes.title: Tracing, Logging & Diagnostics
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: trace flow from ToolUseLoop -> Langfuse handler -> traces (meeseeks-tool-use, meeseeks-task-master, meeseeks-context). Show error path through _safe_execute -> ToolCallResult(success=False). Include the 4-layer investigation methodology: MongoDB → Langfuse → Config → Docker.

page_notes.title: Building a New Client
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: extension flow diagram showing new client -> load_registry(conditional edit tool) + SessionRuntime + permissions -> resolve_session -> run_sync or start_async + poll events. Include project instructions loading (hierarchical CLAUDE.md discovery with noload marker, subtree skills discovery).

page_notes.title: Plugin System & Marketplace
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: plugin lifecycle flow. Discovery: registry_paths scan -> PluginManifest parse -> PluginComponents. Install: marketplace clone/download -> install_path -> manifest write. Session init: load_all_plugin_components() -> fan-out to SkillRegistry.load_extra_dir(), merge_plugin_hooks(), AgentRegistry.register(), extra_mcp_servers. Show security path: path traversal guard -> safe_path check. Show auto-reconcile on startup.

page_notes.title: Agent Definition Registry
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: parse_agent_file() flow: read markdown -> extract YAML frontmatter -> map CC tool names via CC_TOOL_MAP -> AgentDef dataclass -> AgentRegistry.register() (no-override). Show spawn_agent with agent_type -> registry lookup -> apply body/tools/model overrides. Show agent catalog injection into system prompt alongside skill catalog.

page_notes.title: Chat Platform Adapters
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: shared _process_inbound() pipeline (dedup -> mention gate -> session resolve -> slash commands -> LLM). Branch to webhook path (POST /api/webhooks/<platform> -> HMAC verify -> adapter.parse_inbound) and poller path (EmailPoller daemon -> adapter.parse_inbound). Both converge on _process_inbound(). Completion callback reads source_platform from context event -> adapter.send_response(). Show session tag formats for each platform.

page_notes.title: Web IDE Integration
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: request flow. POST /api/sessions/{id}/ide -> IdeManager.ensure() -> Docker SDK spawn (code-server container on meeseeks-ide bridge) -> IdeStore write -> return {url, password}. Nginx ide-proxy routes /ide/<sid>/ via 127.0.0.11 DNS resolver, strips prefix via rewrite. Watchdog sh polls deadline file, self-terminates on expiry. Show three-way bugfix paths: (1) entrypoint chown for bind mount, (2) _container_running() catches exited state, (3) nginx rewrite rule. Console IdeLoader: POST -> poll status -> hidden form submit to /login.

page_notes.title: LSP & Code Intelligence
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: two flows. (1) Passive diagnostics: file edit tool completes -> ToolUseLoop._append_lsp_feedback hook -> LSPTool.get_diagnostics() -> inject as ToolMessage. (2) Explicit lsp_tool call: LLM invokes lsp_tool{operation: 'hover', path, position} -> LSPManager.get_or_spawn_server(language) -> shutil.which discovery -> subprocess spawn -> LSP protocol request -> format_hover()/format_references() -> result. Show graceful absence when LSP_AVAILABLE=False.

page_notes.title: Conversation Fork, Edit & Regenerate
page_notes.notes: Mermaid: fork flow. User clicks ForkFromHereButton at turn T -> POST /api/sessions/{id}/fork{from_ts: T.ts} -> resolve_session(fork_from=id, fork_at_ts=T.ts) -> copy events[0..T] to new session -> return {session_id}. Edit-and-regenerate: user edits user message -> POST /fork{from_ts: T-1.ts, replacement_text: new_text} -> new session with edited message -> start LLM run. Show per-message model override: fork body includes model field -> passed to start_async -> overrides LLMConfig.default_model for this session.
